title: Gymnadenia × wucherpfennigiana
intro: Gymnadenia × wucherpfennigiana, also known as the Wucherpfennig's orchid or hybrid fragrant orchid, is a rare, hybrid terrestrial orchid that is native to Europe. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Gymnadenia × wucherpfennigiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, as well as dried extracts that are used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions.
taxonomy: Gymnadenia × wucherpfennigiana is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the Orchidaceae family. It is a cross between the species Gymnadenia conopsea and Gymnadenia densiflora, resulting in a unique set of characteristics. The exact classification of this hybrid species is still a topic of debate among botanists and orchid enthusiasts.
